intended_use: Template for creating a new disease module. Copy this file, rename module_id and node IDs, replace placeholder rationale and coefficients, and then validate the completed module before use.
missingness_policy: Unobserved inputs are treated as missing/unobserved, not as negative evidence unless a specific negative input node is set to 1.

coefficient_policy.note: Replace template coefficients with disease-specific literature-informed starting values. Do not treat them as validated calibration parameters.

module_role: Template showing the minimum structure for an extensible disease module.

target_diseases.intercept_policy: Placeholder only; configure by target clinic/stage/referral pathway before validation.

input_nodes.label: Example objective laboratory or imaging feature

derived_nodes.description: Intermediate clinical construct that aggregates correlated history/bedside evidence.
Intermediate objective pattern defined by a test threshold or characteristic finding.

edges.evidence_basis: Replace with guideline, cohort, OR/LR, or expert-rationale source.
edges.comments: Raw symptom/history evidence is first aggregated into a syndrome pattern.
Keep downstream diagnosis edge moderate if this finding is already used in the derived node.

edges.evidence_basis: Replace with objective threshold, diagnostic-accuracy result, or consensus criterion.
edges.comments: Objective criteria-defining findings often receive more weight at raw-to-derived placement.
Moderate downstream edge to reduce double counting.
Should not be deterministic unless the disease definition truly requires it.

edges.evidence_basis: Replace with alternative-diagnosis or exclusionary evidence.
edges.comments: Competing evidence should reduce probability without turning the module into a hard rule unless clinically justified.

author_notes: Keep disease-specific knowledge in this JSON file rather than hard-coding it in the inference engine.
Add each new node to node_dictionary.json and stage_config.json after completing the module.
Run: bayesseed validate path/to/completed_module.json before committing.
